stories.title: Bootstrap OpenBook workspace and shared domain model
acceptance_criteria: Repository contains a pnpm workspace with web app, mobile shell, extension scaffold, and shared packages.
Shared types cover library items, document snapshots, import jobs, highlights, notes, and chat threads.
Seed data exists so the reader can be exercised before any import runs.
constraints: Use local-first state for the MVP shell.
risks: Future native parity may require diverging from the web shell.
Large local payloads can stress browser storage.
success_metrics: Web app can load seeded content without a backend database.

stories.title: Implement tablet-first web reader with local notes and sketches
acceptance_criteria: The web app exposes a library route, reader route, and settings route.
Snapshot items render in a paginated reader with TOC navigation.
Text notes and sketch notes can be created and stored in local state.
constraints: Keep note and sketch state in the local OpenBook provider.
Sketch input must work with pointer events.
risks: Selection anchoring can be brittle on complex imported markup.
success_metrics: Reader page renders seeded snapshot content.
Notes and sketches survive refresh via local storage.

stories.title: Implement URL and bundle imports plus book chat route
acceptance_criteria: URL import can detect direct PDFs, attempt DOM extraction, and fall back to a shortcut item.
Authenticated capture bundles can be imported into a snapshot book.
Book chat route uses local retrieval and degrades gracefully without a configured API key.
constraints: Import API stays inside the Next.js app for the current implementation.
AI route must work without requiring persistent backend storage.
risks: OpenAI API responses can fail or change shape.
success_metrics: Import route returns a valid import job payload.
Chat route always returns an assistant message.

stories.title: Verify the shipped OpenBook MVP and generate Ralph evidence
acceptance_criteria: pnpm typecheck passes in the repository root.
pnpm build succeeds for the Next.js web app.
Ralph loop marks this story done and writes evidence artifacts.
non_goals: Add new product features beyond the shipped MVP.
constraints: Use the current repository state as the source of truth.
Do not require AGENTS.md to exist before the run.
risks: Verification can fail if dependency or build state drifts.
Ralph loop may need retries if generated evidence commands fail.
success_metrics: One Ralph loop run completes with OB-004 marked done.
Evidence files are created under stories/OB-004.
